response.justification: The candidate is a strong and versatile senior engineer, but his career centers on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than the deep, current C++ expertise this role demands. His only substantial C++ work was a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, and he shows no exposure to IEC 62443, SDL practices, MQTT, or industrial automation protocols. While his low-latency DLang systems work at Dunnhumby demonstrates transferable systems skills, the gap between his profile and the role's core requirements (modern C++/STL, industrial cybersecurity, message-broker architectures in C++) is too wide to recommend an interview.
strengths: Extensive overall software experience (15+ years) across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering culture background: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: Minimal and dated professional C++ experience (roughly 6 months in 2014–2015)
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or automation protocol experience
Recent focus on full-stack web and AI tooling diverges from the industrial C++ platform domain

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 exposure
